creator: mandree@FreeBSD.org
text:
Am 27.05.2010 19:16, schrieb Anonymous:
> Matthias Andree <mandree@FreeBSD.org> writes:
>
>> I fail to see the problem, only that this would undermine the documented purpose
>> of make.conf, namely affecting the build of the application (as opposed to the
>> result).
>
> That's some new meaning you've got there. Things like setting CC/CXX,
> CFLAGS, using sudo(1) in SU_CMD, using curl/wget in FETCH_CMD, forcing
> make(1) jobs, building with debug symbols or overriding default python
> version are exactly what make.conf is for. It's even described in
> Porter's Handbook and Mk/bsd.port.mk.

/etc/make.conf stuff deals with *build time* configuration,
and *not the installed/runtime result*.

> I for one want to utilize bash emulation in zsh. Why it should be
> restricted? I know exactly what I'm doing.

So you think, but you don't.  You overlooked that overriding BASH_CMD or
thereabouts changes the installed xmlto to depend on zsh, but it will
still record a dependency on bash.  You, or somebody else, may forget
about this unrecorded zsh dependency, deinstall zsh without pkg_delete
complaining, and silently break xmlto.

>> xmlto requires bash and uses the value from BASH_CMD, so we don't want to
>> override anything here, particularly not to zsh.
>
> Even if it does work with zsh without producing errors?

I never tried that, so I have no reason not to believe you that it "works".

That alone is not sufficient for a high-quality and robust port though.

You can always hack the port to suit YOUR needs, but that does not imply
it's suitable for the wide open.

[goal]
> Giving user a choice via well defined place, namely make.conf. I hope
> you're not assuming that user is not responsible for contents of said
> file.

Regardless of the responsibility for your /etc/make.conf, *I* am
responsible that my port records proper dependencies, and your patch
subverts that.  With my port maintainer hat on, my final ruling as to
your proposed patch is "declined".  Sorry.
